Patch Set 1:

Chenfei, FYI this is your first submit. Check the logs and then submit a patch. Looks like the JUnit test for guard failed.

Jorge, please feel free to comment on how the code was restructured. I want to make sure the packages directory is correct and that we get the archetype correct also.

Patch Set 1:

Without having looked at it in detail, the structure looks much more cleaner than before.   A couple questions before I look into it more carefully:

1. I see dir layout as controlloop/common/projects* - the only intermediary directory I can see is "common" under "controlloop.   controlloop is obvious what it means.   What does the "common" category mean?   So if I go with a new control loop project, what are the guidelines to put them under "common" (ie. "controlloop/common/<project>" or to create a new "controlloop/<new-dir>/<project>.

2.  Haven't looked in detail, but do we support or do we have to support old style vFW/vDNS and new one?

Patch Set 1:

> Without having looked at it in detail, the structure looks much
 > more cleaner than before.   A couple questions before I look into
 > it more carefully:
 > 
 > 1. I see dir layout as controlloop/common/projects* - the only
 > intermediary directory I can see is "common" under "controlloop.  
 > controlloop is obvious what it means.   What does the "common"
 > category mean?   So if I go with a new control loop project, what
 > are the guidelines to put them under "common" (ie.
 > "controlloop/common/<project>" or to create a new "controlloop/<new-dir>/<project>.
 > 
 > 2.  Haven't looked in detail, but do we support or do we have to
 > support old style vFW/vDNS and new one?


We have only controlloop use cases as drools applications at the moment, so there is only "controlloop" under "drools-applications". In "controlloop", "common" includes all kinds of common stuffs (e.g. models, actors, eventmanager, guard, yaml SDK, etc) that can be reused by the drools template in "templates". "templates" should include all drools templates supporting controlloop use cases. In this commit, "template.demo.v1.0.0" includes the old style vFW/vDNS template with pattern matches. "template.demo" includes the template we are working on to support vFW/vDNS with AAI integration.

If you go with a new controlloop project, you can put new models needed to "common" if they are not already there, and put your new drools template under "templates" if the existing templates cannot support your new controlloop project.
